thread.title: Congress Stands Strong as Mukesh Malhotra Defeats BJP's Ram Niwas Rawat in Vijaypur Assembly By-Poll | Bhopal News - Times of India
thread.title_full: Congress Stands Strong as Mukesh Malhotra Defeats BJP's Ram Niwas Rawat in Vijaypur Assembly By-Poll | Bhopal News - Times of India

author: Suchandana Gupta
title: Congress Stands Strong as Mukesh Malhotra Defeats BJP's Ram Niwas Rawat in Vijaypur Assembly By-Poll | Bhopal News - Times of India
text: Bhopal: With Saturday's results, it is now concluded that Vijaypur assembly seat in Sheopur district with a predominant 65,000 Sahariya tribal voters is a Congress bastion that the BJPhas failed to conquer. BJP candidate and forest minister Ram Niwas Rawat lost by 7,228 votes against Congress's tribal face Mukesh Malhotra.Since the formation of the state in 1956, there have been 16 elections held in this assembly segment, of which Congress won ten times including this by-poll while the BJP won six times.BJP candidate Rawat, who switched loyalties from the Congress party during the LS elections, won the constituency six times- each time on Cong ticket. It was largely believed that Rawat was influential in the constituency, even Congress rank and file believing him to be the reason behind the party's victories , but voters proved it wrong this time. State BJP chief VD Sharma admitted on Saturday evening, \"We knew Vijaypur was a tooth and nail fight. We will review where we need to improve and work harder in Vijaypur seat and in the elections we will win.\"After results were announced, the BJP asked for a recount. In reply, Congress president Jitu Patwari said, \"Not a single vote can be reduced. I challenge the BJP, CM Mohan Yadav and state BJP president VD Sharma to try manipulating the results.\"Even after joining the BJP, Rawat refused to resign as Congress legislator because he wanted a cabinet berth. No assurances from the BJP could make him resign till July 8, when still as Congress MLA he took oath as minister of the Mohan Yadav govt. After taking oath of office, he put-in his papers, necessitating the bypoll.The Congress fielded Mukesh Malhotra, a Sahariya tribal who was once a BJP worker. Malhotra had quit BJP and joined Congress in 2018. However, he again left Congress and contested as an independent in the 2023 polls. He secured 45,000 votes without the help of any party.The contest was between the OBCs, mainly Rawat's community of the Meenas against the tribals. On the road to the elections, Vijaypur saw a lot of violence, including the attack on a tribal village by a group of dacoits from Rajasthan two days before polling, making it a tough contest on multiple fronts and counts.
